audit_status.source_base: Greek Gospel text tradition, draft aligned to public-domain comparison layer later
audit_status.review_note: Compact complete draft reviewed for mission discourse: twelve apostles, authority, Israel mission, freely received/freely give, hospitality, sheep among wolves, Spirit speech, endurance, fear not, Gehenna, sparrows, confession, sword/division, taking cross, losing/finding life, receiving emissaries.

verses.restored: Calling his twelve disciples, he gave them authority over unclean spirits, to cast them out and to heal every disease and every weakness.
The names of the twelve apostles are these: first Simon, called Peter, and Andrew his brother; James son of Zebedee and John his brother;
Philip and Bartholomew; Thomas and Matthew the tax collector; James son of Alphaeus and Thaddaeus;
Simon the Cananaean/Zealot and Judas Iscariot, who also handed him over.
These twelve Jesus sent out, instructing them, saying, Do not go into a way of nations and do not enter a city of Samaritans,
but go rather to the lost sheep of the house of Israel.
As you go, proclaim, saying, The reign of the heavens has drawn near.
Heal the sick, raise the dead, cleanse those with skin disease, cast out unclean spirits. Freely you received; freely give.
Do not acquire gold, silver, or copper in your belts,
no bag for the way, nor two tunics, sandals, or staff; for the worker is worthy of his food.
Whatever city or village you enter, inquire who in it is worthy, and remain there until you leave.
Entering the house, greet it.
If the house is worthy, let your peace come upon it; but if it is not worthy, let your peace return to you.
Whoever does not receive you or hear your words, as you go out of that house or city, shake off the dust from your feet.
Amen, I say to you: it will be more bearable for the land of Sodom and Gomorrah in the day of judgment than for that city.
Look, I send you out as sheep in the midst of wolves; therefore become wise as serpents and unmixed/innocent as doves.
Beware of people, for they will hand you over to councils and flog you in their synagogues.
You will be brought before governors and kings because of me, as a testimony to them and to the nations.
When they hand you over, do not worry about how or what you should speak, for what you should speak will be given to you in that hour.
For it is not you speaking, but the Spirit of your Father speaking in you.
Brother will hand over brother, and father child; children will rise up against parents and bring about their death.
You will be hated by all because of my name, but the one enduring to the end will be saved.
When they persecute you in this city, flee to the next; for amen, I say to you, you will not finish the cities of Israel until the Son of Humanity comes.
A disciple is not above the teacher, nor a servant above the lord.
It is enough for the disciple to become like the teacher, and the servant like the lord. If they called the master of the house Beelzebul, how much more the members of his household.
Therefore do not fear them; for nothing is covered that will not be unveiled, and hidden that will not be known.
What I tell you in darkness, speak in light; and what you hear in the ear, proclaim on the rooftops.
Do not fear those who kill the body but cannot kill the soul; rather fear the one able to destroy both soul and body in Gehenna.
Are not two sparrows sold for an assarion? Yet not one of them falls to the earth apart from your Father.
Even the hairs of your head are all numbered.
Therefore do not fear; you are worth more than many sparrows.
Everyone who confesses me before people, I also will confess before my Father in the heavens.
Whoever denies me before people, I also will deny before my Father in the heavens.
Do not think that I came to bring peace on the earth; I did not come to bring peace but a sword.
For I came to divide a man against his father, a daughter against her mother, and a daughter-in-law against her mother-in-law.
A person's enemies will be members of his own household.
The one loving father or mother more than me is not worthy of me, and the one loving son or daughter more than me is not worthy of me.
Whoever does not take his cross and follow after me is not worthy of me.
The one finding his life/soul will lose it, and the one losing his life/soul because of me will find it.
The one receiving you receives me, and the one receiving me receives the One who sent me.

The one receiving a prophet in the name of a prophet will receive a prophet's reward, and the one receiving a righteous person in the name of a righteous person will receive a righteous person's reward.
Whoever gives one of these little ones only a cup of cold water in the name of a disciple, amen, I say to you, he will certainly not lose his reward.
